summary: Terminal-based Telegram authentication, implementing the gotd UserAuthenticator interface via stdin/stdout prompts

requires: Existing Telegram account — sign-up is explicitly rejected
guarantees: Phone number is cached after first prompt and reused
Returns a hard error on any sign-up attempt

state: TerminalAuth caches phone after first Phone() call; subsequent calls return cached value without re-prompting

observations.text: SignUp() always returns an error — the system is designed for existing-account login only, not new account registration
AcceptTermsOfService silently accepts without presenting ToS text to the user or confirming consent
AuthFlow interface is a thin re-export of auth.UserAuthenticator — it adds no new surface but decouples client.go from the gotd auth package type directly
prompt() uses fmt.Print (not a logger), mixing authentication interaction with any other stdout output in the process
